cells.cell_text: Number of packed red blood cells transfused

page.content: In Table 1, the qualitative variables show that among 61 seizure-free patients, 37 experienced seizure onset to surgery within ≤1 year, while 17 of the 29 unfavorable patients had the same timing (P-value 0.854). In terms of seizure frequency, 37 seizure-free patients had rare occurrences, compared to 8 unfavorable patients, with a significant P-value of 0.034. Regarding the site of surgery, 34 seizure-free patients underwent left-side surgery, while 17 unfavorable patients did, resulting in a P-value of 0.796. The gender distribution revealed that 31 seizure-free patients were male versus 16 unfavorable males, with a P-value of 0.699. Lastly, 55 seizure-free patients received chemoradiotherapy compared to 24 unfavorable patients, yielding a P-value of 0.319. Table 2 presents various variables with their statistical outcomes; for hemorrhagic shock, the coefficient b is 4.206, standard error se is 1.646, Wald test value is 6.527, degrees of freedom df is 1, odds ratio OR is 67.1, confidence interval for OR is 2.663–1691, and p value is 0.011. For SAPS II score, b is 0.095, se is 0.042, Wald test is 5.092, df is 1, OR is 1.1, 95% CI is 1.013–1.195, and p value is 0.024. Lactate has b 0.262, se 0.149, Wald 3.091, df 1, OR 1.3, CI 0.97–1.741, and p 0.079. Duration of mechanical ventilation shows b 0.052, se 0.032, Wald 2.615, df 1, OR 1.054, CI 0.989–1.123, p 0.106. The number of packed red blood cells transfused has b 0.531, se 0.287, Wald 3.413, df 1, OR 1.701, CI 0.968–2.988, p 0.065. Lastly, conservative treatment reveals b 0.207, se 1.101, Wald 0.035, df 1, OR 1.23, CI 0.142–10.65, and p 0.851.
